plugin.name: dlslab2
plugin.description:
USGS Slab2.0 depth-footprint plugin for DepthLookup.
Provides region-specific default and maximum depths for scautoloc (and other modules) by looking up the event location against USGS Slab2.0 depth-footprint contour polygons.
The dataset covers 30 subduction zones at 20 km depth intervals (000–680 km) generated from Hayes et al. (2018), Slab2.0 (doi:10.5066/F7PV6JNV).
Enable by adding dlslab2 to core.plugins and setting autoloc.depthLookup = Slab2.

parameter.name: directory
parameter.description: Root directory containing Slab2.0 BNA depth-footprint files, organised into 000/, 020/, 040/, ... 680/ subdirectories.

parameter.name: fallback
parameter.description: Default depth returned when the origin is located outside all Slab2.0 depth-footprint polygons and outside any configured polygon regions.

parameter.name: regions
parameter.description:
Ordered, comma-separated list of polygon feature names (from BNA or GeoJSON files in @DATADIR@/spatial/vector/) consulted when the origin falls outside all Slab2.0 zones. Each feature must carry a "defaultDepth" attribute (km) and may carry a "maxDepth" attribute (km). The first matching polygon is used.
This allows region-specific default depths for stable cratons, rifts, or other non-subduction areas on top of the global Slab2.0 coverage.
Example: dlslab2.polygon.regions = australia_craton, african_craton
